target_gpus: NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition (sm_120)
paper_readiness_analysis.paper_title_suggestion: CUDA Tile versus Traditional Kernels: A Comprehensive Performance Evaluation on Blackwell GPUs for Large Language Model Inference

critical_paper_narrative_finding.summary: CuTile does NOT beat cuBLAS/FA2/Triton on RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell — it achieves 53-77% of their performance.
critical_paper_narrative_finding.detail: This is actually a MORE interesting and publishable finding than 'CuTile wins'. The paper narrative should shift from 'CuTile is faster' to 'CuTile provides a significantly simpler programming model (3-5x less code) with reasonable performance, and we analyze WHY it doesn't match tuned baselines yet.'
critical_paper_narrative_finding.key_insight_1: CuTile GEMM achieved only 77% of cuBLAS despite both using Tensor Cores and TMA — the gap comes from cuBLAS's hand-tuned persistent kernels and deep pipelining.
critical_paper_narrative_finding.key_insight_2: CuTile FMHA achieved only 53% of FlashAttention-2 — FA2 has years of optimization on specific attention patterns, while CuTile's compiler is early-stage.
critical_paper_narrative_finding.key_insight_3: CuTile's ByTarget abstraction is LEAKY — missing sm_120 in ByTarget caused a 4x slowdown, undermining the 'write once, run everywhere' claim.
critical_paper_narrative_finding.key_insight_4: CuTile still beat WMMA (1.56x) and cuDNN SDPA (1.35x), showing its value versus non-bleeding-edge baselines.

claim_1.paper_draft: CuTile achieves 9-26% higher prefill throughput than CUTLASS/FlashAttn-2
claim_1.reality: CuTile GEMM is 23% SLOWER than cuBLAS, CuTile FMHA is 47% SLOWER than FA2
claim_1.action: REWRITE: Paper must report honest results. The finding is about productivity vs performance tradeoff.

claim_2.reality: VERIFIED. WMMA: 183 LOC vs CuTile GEMM: 45 LOC = 4.1x reduction. Triton Attn: 87 LOC vs CuTile FMHA: 95 LOC = 0.9x (CuTile is slightly MORE code than Triton for attention).
claim_2.action: Report honestly. CuTile vs CUDA C++ is significant. CuTile vs Triton is a wash.

claim_3.paper_draft: True cross-generation portability (same code on H100 and A100)
claim_3.reality: NOT TESTED. We only ran on RTX PRO 6000. Paper claims A100/H100 — those are not our target GPUs.
claim_3.action: Change to RTX PRO 6000 + B100/B200 portability story.

claim_4.reality: We did NOT benchmark CUTLASS. We used cuBLAS (torch.matmul) and Triton. These are sufficient baselines.
claim_4.action: Remove CUTLASS references or add CUTLASS benchmarks.

claim_5.reality: NOT DONE. Our E2E benchmark uses a custom 4-layer LLaMA-7B model, not vLLM.
claim_5.action: Remove vLLM claims. Our custom model approach is fine but should be described accurately.

claim_6.reality: Only LLaMA-7B config (4 layers) tested. No 13B or Mistral.
claim_6.action: Either add more models or scope paper to LLaMA-7B.

claim_7.reality: NOT DONE. We only have TFLOP/s and time_ms.
claim_7.action: Either add ncu profiling or remove these claims.

claim_8.reality: NOT IMPLEMENTED. No MLP benchmark code exists.
claim_8.action: Either add MLP benchmarks or remove from paper.

critical.name: MLP Fused Kernel Benchmark
critical.description: Paper Section 4.1 mentions MLP kernels (FC1 + SiLU/SwiGLU + FC2). No code or results exist.
critical.effort: HIGH - need to write CuTile MLP kernel + baselines
critical.recommendation: REMOVE from paper scope if time is limited. Our 3 benchmarks (GEMM, Attention, E2E) are sufficient.

important_but_not_blocking.name: Rectangular GEMM shapes
important_but_not_blocking.description: Paper mentions batch×32×4096 × 4096×4096 (FFN projection). We only tested square.
important_but_not_blocking.effort: LOW - just add sizes to MATRIX_SIZES in existing code
important_but_not_blocking.recommendation: ADD. Easy to do, adds value.

important_but_not_blocking.name: Multiple batch sizes for E2E
important_but_not_blocking.description: Paper shows batch=1,8,32 results. We only have batch=1.
important_but_not_blocking.effort: LOW - modify BATCH_SIZE in E2E benchmark

important_but_not_blocking.name: FP16 benchmarks
important_but_not_blocking.description: Paper mentions FP16/BF16. We only have BF16.

nice_to_have.name: Nsight Compute profiling
nice_to_have.description: Roofline analysis, Tensor Core utilization %, memory bandwidth.
nice_to_have.effort: MEDIUM - need ncu on the pod
nice_to_have.recommendation: ADD if time permits. Strong for paper quality.

nice_to_have.name: CUTLASS baselines
nice_to_have.description: Paper mentions CUTLASS 3.2 but we used cuBLAS/Triton instead.
nice_to_have.effort: HIGH - need to set up CUTLASS Python bindings
nice_to_have.recommendation: SKIP. cuBLAS is a stronger baseline than CUTLASS anyway.

nice_to_have.name: Longer sequence lengths
nice_to_have.recommendation: ADD for attention. May need more VRAM.

recommended_paper_rewrite_outline.new_title: Evaluating NVIDIA CuTile: Performance and Productivity Tradeoffs for GPU Kernel Programming on Blackwell Architecture
recommended_paper_rewrite_outline.narrative_shift: From 'CuTile wins' to 'Honest evaluation showing CuTile offers significant code simplicity with a current performance gap, analyzed in depth'

sections.1_Introduction: Motivation: LLM inference needs high-perf kernels. CuTile promises simpler code. We evaluate on Blackwell hardware.
sections.2_Related_Work: Keep mostly as-is. Add CuTile references.
sections.3_Background: CuTile programming model + our experimental setup (RTX PRO 6000 + B100/B200)
sections.4_Methodology: GEMM (5 implementations), Attention (5 implementations), E2E Inference (4 modes). Clear metrics.
sections.5_Results: Tables with TFLOP/s for each. CuTile vs baselines. Honest: CuTile is 53-77% of best baselines.
sections.6_Analysis: WHY CuTile underperforms: compiler maturity, ByTarget leaky abstraction, early-stage runtime. LOC comparison showing 3-5x code reduction.
sections.7_Cross_Architecture: Same code on RTX PRO 6000 vs B100/B200 (when available)
sections.8_Conclusion: CuTile is promising for productivity but not yet performance-competitive with mature libraries. Future compiler improvements could close the gap.

immediate_next_steps: 1. Add rectangular GEMM shapes (4096x11008, 11008x4096) - paper Table 1
3. Run same benchmarks on B100 or B200 for cross-architecture comparison
4. REWRITE paper draft to match actual results (not projected numbers)
5. Add FP16 dtype benchmarks for comparison with BF16
